title: Characterisation of the first 250 000 hospital admissions for COVID-19 in Brazil: a retrospective analysis of nationwide data

openAccessPdf.disclaimer: Notice: Paper or abstract available at https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7834889, which is subject to the license by the author or copyright owner provided with this content. Please go to the source to verify the license and copyright information for your use.

tldr.text: A widespread distribution of COVID-19 was observed across all regions in Brazil, resulting in a high overall disease burden and in-hospital mortality was high, even in patients younger than 60 years, and worsened by existing regional disparities within the health system.
